content: Auto mode lets Claude handle permission prompts automatically — Claude checks each tool call for risky actions and prompt injection before executing. Actions Claude identifies as safe are executed, while actions Claude identifies as risky are blocked and Claude may try a different approach. Ideal for long-running tasks. Sessions are slightly more expensive. Claude can make mistakes that allow harmful commands to run, it's recommended to only use in isolated environments. Shift+Tab to change mode.

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Run a deep verification review — reads full file contents of modified files (not just diffs) to surface integration concerns and regressions in surrounding code.


After running, check the latest file in `.claude/reviews/` for results.

10	## Summary
11	
12	The diff matches the stated intent: fix recursive review triggers and address the ARG_MAX crash in `claude_p_with_timeout`. The file-based lock in `stop.sh`, stale-lock cleanup in `user-prompt-submit.sh`, known-tag sanitization in `extract-turn.sh`, and stdin-delivery fix in `lib-common.sh` all land correctly. The lock logic is the right mechanism given the user's explicit rejection of the prefix-scan approach. However, there are several integration concerns that warrant attention.
13	
14	## Concerns
15	
16	1. **`ORIG_PROMPT` passed as positional arg to `extract-turn.sh` still hits ARG_MAX** — `stop.sh:73` passes `$ORIG_PROMPT` as a shell word on the command line. The ARG_MAX fix was applied to `claude_p_with_timeout` (via stdin tempfile) but not here. A very large prompt (e.g., a wrapup/deepreview prompt that embeds log content) will still trigger "Argument list too long" when calling `extract-turn.sh`. The fix should be consistent: write `$ORIG_PROMPT` to a tempfile and have `extract-turn.sh` read it, or keep the prompt in the JSON turn-state file and re-read it inside the script.
17	
18	2. **`classify_prompt_tags` scans the full prompt in `user-prompt-submit.sh`** — `user-prompt-submit.sh:61` calls `classify_prompt_tags "$PROMPT"` with no length restriction, which means if a sub-session `claude -p` prompt contains embedded log text with a tag, `user-prompt-submit.sh` records `tag_type: "wrapup"` (or similar) in the turn state. `stop.sh` catches this via the lock check and skips the action — so it's functionally correct — but the turn state file contains a misleading `tag_type` for that sub-session. The risk: if the lock times out or fails to be created (race window between lock check and lock write at `stop.sh:90-100`), the sub-session's stale turn state could trigger the action after all.
19	
20	3. **Lock check/create is not atomic** — `stop.sh:90` checks `[ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]`, then `stop.sh:100` does `echo "..." > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"`. Between these two lines, two parallel sub-sessions could both see the lock as absent and both proceed to create it, both triggering actions. In practice this is rare (sub-sessions aren't truly parallel), but the correct pattern would be a conditional `set -o noclobber` write or `mkdir` as an atomic operation.
21	
22	4. **The `#ASSISTANT` block capture issue from the original ask was not confirmed fixed** — The user prompt explicitly asked to "run that `[review]` tuning pass on `extract-turn.sh`" to address `#ASSISTANT` blocks not appearing in logs. The log shows investigation that concluded the blocks "may already be working" but no code change was made to `extract-turn.sh` for this specific behavior, and no confirmed test output showing `#ASSISTANT` blocks captured consistently. The tag sanitization changes were made, but the original ask (assistant-text capture timing fix) is unresolved.
23	
24	5. **`additionalContext` strings in `user-prompt-submit.sh` contain unsanitized hook tags** — Lines 93–106 emit context strings like `"A work-unit wrap-up was requested ([wrapup] detected)."` If these appear in the transcript JSONL (they may not, depending on how `hookSpecificOutput` is stored), `extract-turn.sh` would log them with the literal `[wrapup]` tag, bypassing `sanitize_tags`. The sanitizer only runs on `$ORIG_PROMPT` and `$TEXT` assistant blocks — not on injected context strings.
25	
26	## Verification Notes
27	
28	The stdin/tempfile delivery fix in `lib-common.sh` is correctly implemented and removes the `--bare` flag that was reverted (per WORKFLOW_NOTES). The `sanitize_tags` regex in `extract-turn.sh` correctly targets only named hook tags, not arbitrary brackets, matching the user's explicit instruction. The lock removal inside the background subshell in `stop.sh` is correct — it lives in the `(...)` group that runs before the `&`, so it will execute when the subshell finishes regardless of how long it takes. The 15-minute stale-lock TTL is a known-rough heuristic per WORKFLOW_NOTES.

# Review (deep)

**Generated**: 2026-05-16T05:39:45+00:00
**Log**: `/home/io/io-dev/io/.claude/logs/2026-05-16_general_025143.md`
**Session**: 
**Depth**: deep

---

## Summary

The diff matches the stated intent: fix recursive review triggers and address the ARG_MAX crash in `claude_p_with_timeout`. The file-based lock in `stop.sh`, stale-lock cleanup in `user-prompt-submit.sh`, known-tag sanitization in `extract-turn.sh`, and stdin-delivery fix in `lib-common.sh` all land correctly. The lock logic is the right mechanism given the user's explicit rejection of the prefix-scan approach. However, there are several integration concerns that warrant attention.

## Concerns

1. **`ORIG_PROMPT` passed as positional arg to `extract-turn.sh` still hits ARG_MAX** — `stop.sh:73` passes `$ORIG_PROMPT` as a shell word on the command line. The ARG_MAX fix was applied to `claude_p_with_timeout` (via stdin tempfile) but not here. A very large prompt (e.g., a wrapup/deepreview prompt that embeds log content) will still trigger "Argument list too long" when calling `extract-turn.sh`. The fix should be consistent: write `$ORIG_PROMPT` to a tempfile and have `extract-turn.sh` read it, or keep the prompt in the JSON turn-state file and re-read it inside the script.

2. **`classify_prompt_tags` scans the full prompt in `user-prompt-submit.sh`** — `user-prompt-submit.sh:61` calls `classify_prompt_tags "$PROMPT"` with no length restriction, which means if a sub-session `claude -p` prompt contains embedded log text with a tag, `user-prompt-submit.sh` records `tag_type: "wrapup"` (or similar) in the turn state. `stop.sh` catches this via the lock check and skips the action — so it's functionally correct — but the turn state file contains a misleading `tag_type` for that sub-session. The risk: if the lock times out or fails to be created (race window between lock check and lock write at `stop.sh:90-100`), the sub-session's stale turn state could trigger the action after all.

3. **Lock check/create is not atomic** — `stop.sh:90` checks `[ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]`, then `stop.sh:100` does `echo "..." > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"`. Between these two lines, two parallel sub-sessions could both see the lock as absent and both proceed to create it, both triggering actions. In practice this is rare (sub-sessions aren't truly parallel), but the correct pattern would be a conditional `set -o noclobber` write or `mkdir` as an atomic operation.

4. **The `#ASSISTANT` block capture issue from the original ask was not confirmed fixed** — The user prompt explicitly asked to "run that `[review]` tuning pass on `extract-turn.sh`" to address `#ASSISTANT` blocks not appearing in logs. The log shows investigation that concluded the blocks "may already be working" but no code change was made to `extract-turn.sh` for this specific behavior, and no confirmed test output showing `#ASSISTANT` blocks captured consistently. The tag sanitization changes were made, but the original ask (assistant-text capture timing fix) is unresolved.

5. **`additionalContext` strings in `user-prompt-submit.sh` contain unsanitized hook tags** — Lines 93–106 emit context strings like `"A work-unit wrap-up was requested ([wrapup] detected)."` If these appear in the transcript JSONL (they may not, depending on how `hookSpecificOutput` is stored), `extract-turn.sh` would log them with the literal `[wrapup]` tag, bypassing `sanitize_tags`. The sanitizer only runs on `$ORIG_PROMPT` and `$TEXT` assistant blocks — not on injected context strings.

## Verification Notes

The stdin/tempfile delivery fix in `lib-common.sh` is correctly implemented and removes the `--bare` flag that was reverted (per WORKFLOW_NOTES). The `sanitize_tags` regex in `extract-turn.sh` correctly targets only named hook tags, not arbitrary brackets, matching the user's explicit instruction. The lock removal inside the background subshell in `stop.sh` is correct — it lives in the `(...)` group that runs before the `&`, so it will execute when the subshell finishes regardless of how long it takes. The 15-minute stale-lock TTL is a known-rough heuristic per WORKFLOW_NOTES.

content.text:
Here's the deep review. Five concerns surfaced:

1. **ARG_MAX still hits `extract-turn.sh`** — `stop.sh:73` passes `$ORIG_PROMPT` as a positional arg. The stdin/tempfile fix was only applied inside `claude_p_with_timeout`, not here. A large wrapup/deepreview prompt will still crash with "Argument list too long."

2. **`classify_prompt_tags` runs on full prompt in `user-prompt-submit.sh`** — Sub-sessions with embedded log content can produce a misleading `tag_type` in their turn-state file. Functionally safe while the lock works, but a race window exists between the lock check (line 90) and lock write (line 100) that could allow double-triggering.

3. **Lock check/create is not atomic** — `stop.sh` checks `[ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]` then writes it in two separate steps. Two concurrent sub-sessions could both pass the check before either writes the lock. An atomic `mkdir` or `set -o noclobber` write would close this.

4. **`#ASSISTANT` block capture was not confirmed fixed** — The original ask was to tune `extract-turn.sh` so assistant-text blocks appear in logs consistently. The investigation concluded "may already be working" but no code change was made and no test confirmed it.

5. **`additionalContext` strings in `user-prompt-submit.sh` contain unsanitized hook tags** — The context strings like `"A wrapup was requested ([wrapup] detected)."` aren't run through `sanitize_tags`, so they could appear verbatim in the JSONL transcript with literal bracketed tags.

The highest-priority fix is #1 (ARG_MAX in `extract-turn.sh` call) since it's a confirmed crash path. Want me to fix that first, then tackle the lock atomicity (#3)?

1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# stop.sh — Stop hook
     3	#
     4	# Fires when Claude finishes responding to a turn. Responsibilities:
     5	#   1. Extract the turn's content from the transcript into the work-unit log
     6	#   2. If the prompt for this turn had [wrapup], trigger wrap-up sequence
     7	#   3. If [review], trigger review only
     8	#   4. If [deepreview], trigger deepreview
     9	#   5. If [docfresh:slug], trigger targeted doc refresh
    10	#   6. Clean up turn state
    11	#
    12	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
    13	#   - session_id, transcript_path, hook_event_name, stop_hook_active
    14	#
    15	# Output:
    16	#   - exit 0 always; we never block Claude from finishing
    17	
    18	set -uo pipefail
    19	
    20	HOOK_NAME="stop"
    21	export HOOK_NAME
    22	
    23	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
    24	if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    25	    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
    26	    exit 0
    27	fi
    28	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
    29	source "$LIB_PATH"
    30	
    31	INPUT=$(cat)
    32	
    33	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
    34	TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
    35	STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false')
    36	
    37	export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
    38	ensure_workflow_dirs
    39	
    40	# Lock file used to prevent recursive action triggers from claude -p sub-sessions.
    41	# When a review/wrapup/etc. spawns claude -p, that sub-session's Stop hook would
    42	# re-detect tags embedded in the log content and trigger another action. The lock
    43	# blocks that. Stale locks (older than 15 min) are cleaned up by user-prompt-submit.sh.
    44	WORKFLOW_LOCK="${WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR}/workflow-action.lock"
    45	
    46	if [ "$STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE" = "true" ]; then
    47	    hook_debug "stop_hook_active=true, skipping to avoid loop"
    48	    exit 0
    49	fi
    50	
    51	# Read turn state recorded by UserPromptSubmit
    52	STATE=$(read_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null) || {
    53	    hook_debug "no turn state found for session $SESSION_ID; skipping extraction"
    54	    exit 0
    55	}
    56	
    57	TAG_TYPE=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.tag_type // "none"')
    58	LOG_PATH=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.log_path // ""')
    59	ORIG_PROMPT=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
    60	TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.transcript_offset // 0')
    61	
    62	hook_debug "Stop hook: tag_type=$TAG_TYPE log=$LOG_PATH"
    63	
    64	# ============================================================================
    65	# STEP 1: Extract turn from transcript into log
    66	# ============================================================================
    67	
    68	if [ -z "$LOG_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    69	    hook_debug "Cannot extract: log=$LOG_PATH transcript=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
    70	else
    71	    EXTRACT_SCRIPT="${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/extract-turn.sh"
    72	    if [ -x "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" ]; then
    73	        "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET" "$LOG_PATH" "$ORIG_PROMPT" 2>>"$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" || {
    74	            hook_debug "extract-turn.sh failed (exit $?)"
    75	        }
    76	    else
    77	        hook_debug "extract-turn.sh not found or not executable at $EXTRACT_SCRIPT"
    78	    fi
    79	fi
    80	
    81	# ============================================================================
    82	# STEP 2: Tag-driven follow-up actions
    83	# ============================================================================
    84	
    85	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    86	    wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh)
    87	        # Check lock before triggering any LLM action. If held, this is a
    88	        # claude -p sub-session whose prompt contains an embedded tag from the
    89	        # work-unit log. Skip the action entirely.
    90	        if [ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]; then
    91	            hook_debug "workflow-action.lock held — skipping $TAG_TYPE (sub-session recursion guard)"
    92	            clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
    93	            exit 0
    94	        fi
    95	        ;;
    96	esac
    97	
    98	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    99	    wrapup)
   100	        echo "$$:wrapup:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   101	        hook_debug "Triggering wrap-up sequence"
   102	        (
   103	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/generate-summary.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
   104	            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW" = "1" ]; then
   105	                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
   106	            fi
   107	            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS" = "1" ]; then
   108	                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
   109	            fi
   110	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   111	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/wrapup.log" 2>&1 &
   112	        hook_debug "wrap-up started in background, pid=$!"
   113	        jq -nc \
   114	            --arg ctx "Work-unit wrap-up sequence started in background. Results will appear in .claude/summaries/, .claude/reviews/, and .claude/docs/interim/ when complete." \
   115	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   116	        ;;
   117	
   118	    review)
   119	        echo "$$:review:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   120	        hook_debug "Triggering shallow review"
   121	        (
   122	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
   123	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   124	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
   125	        hook_debug "review started in background, pid=$!"
   126	        jq -nc \
   127	            --arg ctx "Shallow review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
   128	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   129	        ;;
   130	
   131	    deepreview)
   132	        echo "$$:deepreview:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   133	        hook_debug "Triggering deep review"
   134	        (
   135	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "deep"
   136	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   137	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
   138	        hook_debug "deep review started in background, pid=$!"
   139	        jq -nc \
   140	            --arg ctx "Deep review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
   141	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   142	        ;;
   143	
   144	    docfresh)
   145	        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$ORIG_PROMPT")
   146	        echo "$$:docfresh:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   147	        hook_debug "Triggering docfresh for slug=$SLUG"
   148	        (
   149	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "$SLUG"
   150	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   151	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/docfresh.log" 2>&1 &
   152	        hook_debug "docfresh started in background, pid=$!"
   153	        jq -nc \
   154	            --arg ctx "Documentation refresh for '$SLUG' started in background. Check .claude/docs/interim/$SLUG.md when complete." \
   155	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   156	        ;;
   157	
   158	    *)
   159	        # No follow-up actions for plain prompts, initprompt, or phaseprompt
   160	        ;;
   161	esac
   162	
   163	# ============================================================================
   164	# STEP 3: Clean up turn state
   165	# ============================================================================
   166	
   167	clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
   168	
   169	exit 0

toolUseResult.stdout:
1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# stop.sh — Stop hook
     3	#
     4	# Fires when Claude finishes responding to a turn. Responsibilities:
     5	#   1. Extract the turn's content from the transcript into the work-unit log
     6	#   2. If the prompt for this turn had [wrapup], trigger wrap-up sequence
     7	#   3. If [review], trigger review only
     8	#   4. If [deepreview], trigger deepreview
     9	#   5. If [docfresh:slug], trigger targeted doc refresh
    10	#   6. Clean up turn state
    11	#
    12	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
    13	#   - session_id, transcript_path, hook_event_name, stop_hook_active
    14	#
    15	# Output:
    16	#   - exit 0 always; we never block Claude from finishing
    17	
    18	set -uo pipefail
    19	
    20	HOOK_NAME="stop"
    21	export HOOK_NAME
    22	
    23	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
    24	if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    25	    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
    26	    exit 0
    27	fi
    28	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
    29	source "$LIB_PATH"
    30	
    31	INPUT=$(cat)
    32	
    33	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
    34	TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
    35	STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false')
    36	
    37	export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
    38	ensure_workflow_dirs
    39	
    40	# Lock file used to prevent recursive action triggers from claude -p sub-sessions.
    41	# When a review/wrapup/etc. spawns claude -p, that sub-session's Stop hook would
    42	# re-detect tags embedded in the log content and trigger another action. The lock
    43	# blocks that. Stale locks (older than 15 min) are cleaned up by user-prompt-submit.sh.
    44	WORKFLOW_LOCK="${WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR}/workflow-action.lock"
    45	
    46	if [ "$STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE" = "true" ]; then
    47	    hook_debug "stop_hook_active=true, skipping to avoid loop"
    48	    exit 0
    49	fi
    50	
    51	# Read turn state recorded by UserPromptSubmit
    52	STATE=$(read_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null) || {
    53	    hook_debug "no turn state found for session $SESSION_ID; skipping extraction"
    54	    exit 0
    55	}
    56	
    57	TAG_TYPE=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.tag_type // "none"')
    58	LOG_PATH=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.log_path // ""')
    59	ORIG_PROMPT=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
    60	TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.transcript_offset // 0')
    61	
    62	hook_debug "Stop hook: tag_type=$TAG_TYPE log=$LOG_PATH"
    63	
    64	# ============================================================================
    65	# STEP 1: Extract turn from transcript into log
    66	# ============================================================================
    67	
    68	if [ -z "$LOG_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    69	    hook_debug "Cannot extract: log=$LOG_PATH transcript=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
    70	else
    71	    EXTRACT_SCRIPT="${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/extract-turn.sh"
    72	    if [ -x "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" ]; then
    73	        "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET" "$LOG_PATH" "$ORIG_PROMPT" 2>>"$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" || {
    74	            hook_debug "extract-turn.sh failed (exit $?)"
    75	        }
    76	    else
    77	        hook_debug "extract-turn.sh not found or not executable at $EXTRACT_SCRIPT"
    78	    fi
    79	fi
    80	
    81	# ============================================================================
    82	# STEP 2: Tag-driven follow-up actions
    83	# ============================================================================
    84	
    85	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    86	    wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh)
    87	        # Check lock before triggering any LLM action. If held, this is a
    88	        # claude -p sub-session whose prompt contains an embedded tag from the
    89	        # work-unit log. Skip the action entirely.
    90	        if [ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]; then
    91	            hook_debug "workflow-action.lock held — skipping $TAG_TYPE (sub-session recursion guard)"
    92	            clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
    93	            exit 0
    94	        fi
    95	        ;;
    96	esac
    97	
    98	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    99	    wrapup)
   100	        echo "$$:wrapup:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   101	        hook_debug "Triggering wrap-up sequence"
   102	        (
   103	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/generate-summary.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
   104	            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW" = "1" ]; then
   105	                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
   106	            fi
   107	            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS" = "1" ]; then
   108	                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
   109	            fi
   110	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   111	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/wrapup.log" 2>&1 &
   112	        hook_debug "wrap-up started in background, pid=$!"
   113	        jq -nc \
   114	            --arg ctx "Work-unit wrap-up sequence started in background. Results will appear in .claude/summaries/, .claude/reviews/, and .claude/docs/interim/ when complete." \
   115	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   116	        ;;
   117	
   118	    review)
   119	        echo "$$:review:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   120	        hook_debug "Triggering shallow review"
   121	        (
   122	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
   123	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   124	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
   125	        hook_debug "review started in background, pid=$!"
   126	        jq -nc \
   127	            --arg ctx "Shallow review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
   128	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   129	        ;;
   130	
   131	    deepreview)
   132	        echo "$$:deepreview:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   133	        hook_debug "Triggering deep review"
   134	        (
   135	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "deep"
   136	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   137	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
   138	        hook_debug "deep review started in background, pid=$!"
   139	        jq -nc \
   140	            --arg ctx "Deep review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
   141	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   142	        ;;
   143	
   144	    docfresh)
   145	        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$ORIG_PROMPT")
   146	        echo "$$:docfresh:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   147	        hook_debug "Triggering docfresh for slug=$SLUG"
   148	        (
   149	            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "$SLUG"
   150	            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
   151	        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/docfresh.log" 2>&1 &
   152	        hook_debug "docfresh started in background, pid=$!"
   153	        jq -nc \
   154	            --arg ctx "Documentation refresh for '$SLUG' started in background. Check .claude/docs/interim/$SLUG.md when complete." \
   155	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   156	        ;;
   157	
   158	    *)
   159	        # No follow-up actions for plain prompts, initprompt, or phaseprompt
   160	        ;;
   161	esac
   162	
   163	# ============================================================================
   164	# STEP 3: Clean up turn state
   165	# ============================================================================
   166	
   167	clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
   168	
   169	exit 0

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# extract-turn.sh — Pull a single turn out of the transcript JSONL into the
     3	# work-unit log with structural tags.
     4	#
     5	# Usage: extract-turn.sh TRANSCRIPT_PATH START_OFFSET LOG_PATH ORIG_PROMPT
     6	#
     7	# Reads entries from TRANSCRIPT_PATH starting at byte offset START_OFFSET,
     8	# parses each JSONL line, filters and tags them, and appends to LOG_PATH.
     9	#
    10	# This is the "extraction" we discussed — converts noisy transcript into
    11	# clean tagged log entries. Structural tags only (no LLM analysis).
    12	
    13	set -uo pipefail
    14	
    15	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
    16	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    17	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    18	    source "$LIB_PATH"
    19	fi
    20	
    21	TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$1"
    22	START_OFFSET="$2"
    23	LOG_PATH="$3"
    24	ORIG_PROMPT="$4"
    25	
    26	# Sanitize workflow tags so they don't re-trigger hooks when log content is
    27	# embedded verbatim in claude -p review/summary prompts. Converts [tag] to
    28	# ~tag~ — still readable in the log, but won't match tag detection patterns.
    29	sanitize_tags() {
    30	    sed -E 's/\[(initprompt|phaseprompt|wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh:[a-z0-9-]*)\]/~\1~/g'
    31	}
    32	
    33	if [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    34	    echo "extract-turn: transcript not found: $TRANSCRIPT_PATH" >&2
    35	    exit 1
    36	fi
    37	
    38	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    39	    echo "extract-turn: log file not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    40	    exit 1
    41	fi
    42	
    43	# Read the transcript content starting at START_OFFSET
    44	# Use tail -c to skip to the byte offset
    45	TRANSCRIPT_SLICE=$(tail -c +"$((START_OFFSET + 1))" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" 2>/dev/null)
    46	
    47	if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" ]; then
    48	    # Nothing new in transcript; just append a marker for the prompt
    49	    {
    50	        echo ""
    51	        echo "## #PROMPT"
    52	        echo ""
    53	        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    54	        echo ""
    55	        echo "\`\`\`"
    56	        echo "$ORIG_PROMPT"
    57	        echo "\`\`\`"
    58	        echo ""
    59	        echo "_(No new transcript entries since prompt submission)_"
    60	        echo ""
    61	    } >> "$LOG_PATH"
    62	    exit 0
    63	fi
    64	
    65	# ============================================================================
    66	# Parse JSONL entries and append structural-tagged entries to the log
    67	# ============================================================================
    68	
    69	# Append the prompt header
    70	{
    71	    echo ""
    72	    echo "## #PROMPT"
    73	    echo ""
    74	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    75	    echo ""
    76	    echo "\`\`\`"
    77	    echo "$ORIG_PROMPT" | sanitize_tags
    78	    echo "\`\`\`"
    79	    echo ""
    80	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
    81	
    82	# Process each line of the transcript slice. Each line is a JSON object.
    83	# We're looking for specific entry types and extracting useful content.
    84	#
    85	# The transcript JSONL format from Claude Code contains entries like:
    86	#   { "type": "user", "message": {...} }
    87	#   { "type": "assistant", "message": { "content": [...] } }
    88	#   where content items have types: text, tool_use, tool_result, thinking
    89	#
    90	# We extract:
    91	#   - assistant text → #ASSISTANT
    92	#   - tool_use (Bash) → #BASH
    93	#   - tool_use (Edit/Write/MultiEdit) → already covered by PostToolUse hook
    94	#   - tool_use (Grep) → #GREP
    95	#   - tool_use (Glob) → #GLOB
    96	#   - tool_use (Read) → skipped (too noisy)
    97	#   - tool_use (TodoWrite) → #TODO
    98	#   - tool_use (Task) → #TASK
    99	#   - tool_use (WebSearch/WebFetch) → #WEB
   100	#   - tool_result with is_error=true → #ERROR
   101	#
   102	# We deliberately skip routine tool calls that don't add information.
   103	
   104	echo "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" | while IFS= read -r line; do
   105	    # Skip empty lines
   106	    if [ -z "$line" ]; then continue; fi
   107	
   108	    # Skip lines that aren't valid JSON
   109	    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then continue; fi
   110	
   111	    ENTRY_TYPE=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // ""')
   112	
   113	    case "$ENTRY_TYPE" in
   114	        assistant)
   115	            # Extract content items from the assistant message
   116	            # Iterate through .message.content array
   117	            CONTENT_ITEMS=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // [] | .[]' 2>/dev/null)
   118	
   119	            echo "$CONTENT_ITEMS" | while IFS= read -r item; do
   120	                if [ -z "$item" ]; then continue; fi
   121	                ITEM_TYPE=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type // ""')
   122	
   123	                case "$ITEM_TYPE" in
   124	                    text)
   125	                        TEXT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.text // ""')
   126	                        if [ -n "$TEXT" ]; then
   127	                            {
   128	                                echo ""
   129	                                echo "## #ASSISTANT"
   130	                                echo ""
   131	                                echo "$TEXT" | sanitize_tags
   132	                                echo ""
   133	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   134	                        fi
   135	                        ;;
   136	                    tool_use)
   137	                        TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.name // ""')
   138	                        case "$TOOL_NAME" in
   139	                            Bash)
   140	                                CMD=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.command // ""')
   141	                                DESC=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.description // ""')
   142	                                {
   143	                                    echo ""
   144	                                    echo "## #BASH"
   145	                                    [ -n "$DESC" ] && echo "_${DESC}_"
   146	                                    echo ""
   147	                                    echo "\`\`\`bash"
   148	                                    echo "$CMD"
   149	                                    echo "\`\`\`"
   150	                                    echo ""
   151	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   152	                                ;;
   153	                            Grep)
   154	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
   155	                                PATH_ARG=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.path // ""')
   156	                                {
   157	                                    echo ""
   158	                                    echo "## #GREP \`$PATTERN\` in \`$PATH_ARG\`"
   159	                                    echo ""
   160	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   161	                                ;;
   162	                            Glob)
   163	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
   164	                                {
   165	                                    echo ""
   166	                                    echo "## #GLOB \`$PATTERN\`"
   167	                                    echo ""
   168	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   169	                                ;;
   170	                            TodoWrite)
   171	                                {
   172	                                    echo ""
   173	                                    echo "## #TODO Todo list updated"
   174	                                    echo ""
   175	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   176	                                ;;
   177	                            Task|Agent)
   178	                                SUBAGENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.subagent_type // .input.description // ""')
   179	                                {
   180	                                    echo ""
   181	                                    echo "## #TASK Subagent: $SUBAGENT"
   182	                                    echo ""
   183	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   184	                                ;;
   185	                            WebSearch|WebFetch)
   186	                                QUERY=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.query // .input.url // ""')
   187	                                {
   188	                                    echo ""
   189	                                    echo "## #WEB $TOOL_NAME: $QUERY"
   190	                                    echo ""
   191	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   192	                                ;;
   193	                            # Edit, Write, MultiEdit are logged by post-tool-use.sh
   194	                            # Read is intentionally skipped as too noisy
   195	                            *) ;;
   196	                        esac
   197	                        ;;
   198	                    tool_result)
   199	                        IS_ERROR=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
   200	                        if [ "$IS_ERROR" = "true" ]; then
   201	                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.content // "" | if type == "string" then . else (tostring) end')
   202	                            # Truncate very long errors
   203	                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$ERR_CONTENT" | head -c 2000)
   204	                            {
   205	                                echo ""
   206	                                echo "## #ERROR"
   207	                                echo ""
   208	                                echo "\`\`\`"
   209	                                echo "$ERR_CONTENT"
   210	                                echo "\`\`\`"
   211	                                echo ""
   212	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   213	                        fi
   214	                        ;;
   215	                    thinking)
   216	                        # Skip thinking blocks — too verbose, not load-bearing for review
   217	                        ;;
   218	                esac
   219	            done
   220	            ;;
   221	        user)
   222	            # The user message is already captured at the top (#PROMPT).
   223	            # Skip subsequent user messages (these are typically tool results being
   224	            # threaded back to the model, not new user input).
   225	            ;;
   226	        system)
   227	            # Skip system messages
   228	            ;;
   229	    esac
   230	done
   231	
   232	# Append a turn-end marker
   233	{
   234	    echo ""
   235	    echo "## #TURN_END"
   236	    echo ""
   237	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
   238	    echo ""
   239	    echo "---"
   240	    echo ""
   241	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
   242	
   243	exit 0

   120	                if [ -z "$item" ]; then continue; fi
   121	                ITEM_TYPE=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type // ""')
   122	
   123	                case "$ITEM_TYPE" in
   124	                    text)
   125	                        TEXT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.text // ""')
   126	                        if [ -n "$TEXT" ]; then
   127	                            {
   128	                                echo ""
   129	                                echo "## #ASSISTANT"
   130	                                echo ""
   131	                                echo "$TEXT" | sanitize_tags
   132	                                echo ""
   133	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   134	                        fi
   135	                        ;;
   136	                    tool_use)
   137	                        TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.name // ""')
   138	                        case "$TOOL_NAME" in
   139	                            Bash)
   140	                                CMD=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.command // ""')
   141	                                DESC=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.description // ""')
   142	                                {
   143	                                    echo ""
   144	                                    echo "## #BASH"
   145	                                    [ -n "$DESC" ] && echo "_${DESC}_"
   146	                                    echo ""
   147	                                    echo "\`\`\`bash"
   148	                                    echo "$CMD"
   149	                                    echo "\`\`\`"
   150	                                    echo ""
   151	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   152	                                ;;
   153	                            Grep)
   154	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
   155	                                PATH_ARG=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.path // ""')
   156	                                {
   157	                                    echo ""
   158	                                    echo "## #GREP \`$PATTERN\` in \`$PATH_ARG\`"
   159	                                    echo ""
   160	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   161	                                ;;
   162	                            Glob)
   163	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
   164	                                {
   165	                                    echo ""
   166	                                    echo "## #GLOB \`$PATTERN\`"
   167	                                    echo ""
   168	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   169	                                ;;
   170	                            TodoWrite)
   171	                                {
   172	                                    echo ""
   173	                                    echo "## #TODO Todo list updated"
   174	                                    echo ""
   175	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   176	                                ;;
   177	                            Task|Agent)
   178	                                SUBAGENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.subagent_type // .input.description // ""')
   179	                                {
   180	                                    echo ""
   181	                                    echo "## #TASK Subagent: $SUBAGENT"
   182	                                    echo ""
   183	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   184	                                ;;
   185	                            WebSearch|WebFetch)
   186	                                QUERY=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.query // .input.url // ""')
   187	                                {
   188	                                    echo ""
   189	                                    echo "## #WEB $TOOL_NAME: $QUERY"
   190	                                    echo ""
   191	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   192	                                ;;
   193	                            # Edit, Write, MultiEdit are logged by post-tool-use.sh
   194	                            # Read is intentionally skipped as too noisy
   195	                            *) ;;
   196	                        esac
   197	                        ;;
   198	                    tool_result)
   199	                        IS_ERROR=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
   200	                        if [ "$IS_ERROR" = "true" ]; then
   201	                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.content // "" | if type == "string" then . else (tostring) end')
   202	                            # Truncate very long errors
   203	                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$ERR_CONTENT" | head -c 2000)
   204	                            {
   205	                                echo ""
   206	                                echo "## #ERROR"
   207	                                echo ""
   208	                                echo "\`\`\`"
   209	                                echo "$ERR_CONTENT"
   210	                                echo "\`\`\`"
   211	                                echo ""
   212	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
   213	                        fi
   214	                        ;;
   215	                    thinking)
   216	                        # Skip thinking blocks — too verbose, not load-bearing for review
   217	                        ;;
   218	                esac
   219	            done
   220	            ;;
   221	        user)
   222	            # The user message is already captured at the top (#PROMPT).
   223	            # Skip subsequent user messages (these are typically tool results being
   224	            # threaded back to the model, not new user input).
   225	            ;;
   226	        system)
   227	            # Skip system messages
   228	            ;;
   229	    esac
   230	done
   231	
   232	# Append a turn-end marker
   233	{
   234	    echo ""
   235	    echo "## #TURN_END"
   236	    echo ""
   237	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
   238	    echo ""
   239	    echo "---"
   240	    echo ""
   241	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
   242	
   243	exit 0

1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# user-prompt-submit.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook
     3	#
     4	# Fires on every prompt submission. Responsibilities:
     5	#   1. Detect workflow tags in the prompt
     6	#   2. Rotate the work-unit log if this is a new work unit ([initprompt])
     7	#   3. Record turn state so the Stop hook knows what just happened
     8	#   4. Optionally inject context for Claude about which tags it saw
     9	#
    10	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
    11	#   - session_id, transcript_path, cwd, permission_mode, prompt
    12	#
    13	# Output:
    14	#   - exit 0 with no JSON: prompt proceeds normally
    15	#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: prompt proceeds, context added
    16	#   - We deliberately do NOT block prompts with this hook
    17	
    18	set -uo pipefail
    19	
    20	HOOK_NAME="user-prompt-submit"
    21	export HOOK_NAME
    22	
    23	# Source the common library (path resolved via CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR)
    24	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
    25	if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    26	    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
    27	    exit 1
    28	fi
    29	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
    30	source "$LIB_PATH"
    31	
    32	# Read JSON input from stdin
    33	INPUT=$(cat)
    34	
    35	# Extract fields
    36	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
    37	TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
    38	PROMPT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
    39	
    40	export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
    41	
    42	# Ensure directory structure exists (idempotent)
    43	ensure_workflow_dirs
    44	
    45	# Clean stale workflow-action lock. Normally removed by the background subshell
    46	# in stop.sh when the action completes. If that process crashed or timed out,
    47	# the lock stays until the next real user prompt clears it.
    48	# 15 min exceeds the longest possible wrapup (3-4 claude -p calls × 180s timeout).
    49	WORKFLOW_LOCK="${WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR}/workflow-action.lock"
    50	if [ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]; then
    51	    lock_age=$(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) ))
    52	    if [ "$lock_age" -gt 900 ]; then
    53	        rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
    54	        hook_debug "Cleaned stale workflow-action.lock (age: ${lock_age}s)"
    55	    fi
    56	fi
    57	
    58	hook_debug "session=$SESSION_ID prompt_length=${#PROMPT}"
    59	
    60	# Classify the prompt
    61	TAG_TYPE=$(classify_prompt_tags "$PROMPT")
    62	hook_debug "tag_type=$TAG_TYPE"
    63	
    64	# Rotate log if this is a new work unit
    65	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    66	    init)
    67	        # Extract a descriptor from the prompt (first 50 chars, slugified)
    68	        # User can include something hint-y after [initprompt] to name the work unit
    69	        DESCRIPTOR_TEXT=$(echo "$PROMPT" | sed -E 's/\[initprompt\]//' | head -c 80)
    70	        DESCRIPTOR=$(slugify "$DESCRIPTOR_TEXT")
    71	        if [ -z "$DESCRIPTOR" ]; then
    72	            DESCRIPTOR="work-unit"
    73	        fi
    74	        # Cap the descriptor length to keep filenames sane
    75	        DESCRIPTOR=$(echo "$DESCRIPTOR" | head -c 40)
    76	        rotate_log_to_new "$DESCRIPTOR" > /dev/null
    77	        hook_debug "rotated log on initprompt with descriptor=$DESCRIPTOR"
    78	        ;;
    79	    *)
    80	        # No rotation for other tag types; current log continues
    81	        # If no current log exists, get_current_log_path will create one
    82	        get_current_log_path > /dev/null
    83	        ;;
    84	esac
    85	
    86	# Record turn state for the Stop hook
    87	record_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" "$PROMPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
    88	
    89	# Optionally inject context for Claude about what's happening
    90	# This is useful when a tag triggers special behavior, so Claude knows
    91	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    92	    init)
    93	        CONTEXT="A new work unit was started ([initprompt] detected). A fresh work-unit log was created. Proceed with the task as normal; the workflow infrastructure will capture the work."
    94	        ;;
    95	    wrapup)
    96	        CONTEXT="A work-unit wrap-up was requested ([wrapup] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will automatically generate a summary, run a review of work done since the last [initprompt], and propose documentation updates. Proceed with any final steps for this work unit."
    97	        ;;
    98	    review)
    99	        CONTEXT="A mid-work-unit review was requested ([review] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will run a review of work done since the last [initprompt] and surface any concerns. Proceed with the task; the review runs separately."
   100	        ;;
   101	    deepreview)
   102	        CONTEXT="A deep review was requested ([deepreview] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will run an in-depth review including full file contents of changed code. Proceed with the task; the review runs separately."
   103	        ;;
   104	    docfresh)
   105	        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$PROMPT")
   106	        CONTEXT="A targeted documentation refresh was requested ([docfresh:$SLUG] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will update the interim doc for '$SLUG' based on recent work. Proceed with the task."
   107	        ;;
   108	    *)
   109	        # No context injection for plain prompts or phase prompts
   110	        exit 0
   111	        ;;
   112	esac
   113	
   114	# Emit context JSON
   115	jq -nc \
   116	    --arg ctx "$CONTEXT" \
   117	    '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "UserPromptSubmit", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
   118	
   119	exit 0

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. Three fixes:
1. **ARG_MAX**: Pass prompt via tempfile to `extract-turn.sh` instead of as a positional arg
2. **Lock atomicity**: Single noclobber write replaces the check+create two-step
3. **JSON validation**: Stop hook's `hookSpecificOutput` format is invalid for the Stop event — just remove it (the background work is already kicked off; the status message is noise)

        rm -f "$PROMPT_TMP"
    else
        hook_debug "extract-turn.sh not found or not executable at $EXTRACT_SCRIPT"
    fi
fi

#!/bin/bash
# stop.sh — Stop hook
#
# Fires when Claude finishes responding to a turn. Responsibilities:
#   1. Extract the turn's content from the transcript into the work-unit log
#   2. If the prompt for this turn had [wrapup], trigger wrap-up sequence
#   3. If [review], trigger review only
#   4. If [deepreview], trigger deepreview
#   5. If [docfresh:slug], trigger targeted doc refresh
#   6. Clean up turn state
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - session_id, transcript_path, hook_event_name, stop_hook_active
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 always; we never block Claude from finishing

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="stop"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
    exit 0
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false')

export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
ensure_workflow_dirs

# Lock file used to prevent recursive action triggers from claude -p sub-sessions.
# When a review/wrapup/etc. spawns claude -p, that sub-session's Stop hook would
# re-detect tags embedded in the log content and trigger another action. The lock
# blocks that. Stale locks (older than 15 min) are cleaned up by user-prompt-submit.sh.
WORKFLOW_LOCK="${WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR}/workflow-action.lock"

if [ "$STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE" = "true" ]; then
    hook_debug "stop_hook_active=true, skipping to avoid loop"
    exit 0
fi

# Read turn state recorded by UserPromptSubmit
STATE=$(read_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null) || {
    hook_debug "no turn state found for session $SESSION_ID; skipping extraction"
    exit 0
}

TAG_TYPE=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.tag_type // "none"')
LOG_PATH=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.log_path // ""')
ORIG_PROMPT=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.transcript_offset // 0')

hook_debug "Stop hook: tag_type=$TAG_TYPE log=$LOG_PATH"

# ============================================================================
# STEP 1: Extract turn from transcript into log
# ============================================================================

if [ -z "$LOG_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    hook_debug "Cannot extract: log=$LOG_PATH transcript=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
else
    EXTRACT_SCRIPT="${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/extract-turn.sh"
    if [ -x "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" ]; then
        "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET" "$LOG_PATH" "$ORIG_PROMPT" 2>>"$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" || {
            hook_debug "extract-turn.sh failed (exit $?)"
        }
    else
        hook_debug "extract-turn.sh not found or not executable at $EXTRACT_SCRIPT"
    fi
fi

# ============================================================================
# STEP 2: Tag-driven follow-up actions
# ============================================================================

case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh)
        # Check lock before triggering any LLM action. If held, this is a
        # claude -p sub-session whose prompt contains an embedded tag from the
        # work-unit log. Skip the action entirely.
        if [ -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK" ]; then
            hook_debug "workflow-action.lock held — skipping $TAG_TYPE (sub-session recursion guard)"
            clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
            exit 0
        fi
        ;;
esac

case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup)
        echo "$$:wrapup:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        hook_debug "Triggering wrap-up sequence"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/generate-summary.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            fi
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            fi
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/wrapup.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "wrap-up started in background, pid=$!"
        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Work-unit wrap-up sequence started in background. Results will appear in .claude/summaries/, .claude/reviews/, and .claude/docs/interim/ when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    review)
        echo "$$:review:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        hook_debug "Triggering shallow review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "review started in background, pid=$!"
        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Shallow review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    deepreview)
        echo "$$:deepreview:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        hook_debug "Triggering deep review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "deep"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "deep review started in background, pid=$!"
        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Deep review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    docfresh)
        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$ORIG_PROMPT")
        echo "$$:docfresh:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        hook_debug "Triggering docfresh for slug=$SLUG"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "$SLUG"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/docfresh.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "docfresh started in background, pid=$!"
        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Documentation refresh for '$SLUG' started in background. Check .claude/docs/interim/$SLUG.md when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    *)
        # No follow-up actions for plain prompts, initprompt, or phaseprompt
        ;;
esac

# ============================================================================
# STEP 3: Clean up turn state
# ============================================================================

clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"

exit 0

lines: +        # Write prompt to a tempfile to avoid ARG_MAX when the prompt is large
+        # (e.g. wrapup/deepreview prompts that embed log content).
hook_debug "extract-turn.sh not found or not executable at $EXTRACT_SCRIPT"

input.new_string:
case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh)
        # Atomic lock acquire: noclobber makes the existence-check and create a
        # single syscall (EEXIST on collision), closing the TOCTOU race window.
        # If the lock is held this is a claude -p sub-session whose prompt
        # contains an embedded tag from the work-unit log — skip the action.
        if ! (set -o noclobber; echo "$$:$TAG_TYPE:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK") 2>/dev/null; then
            hook_debug "workflow-action.lock held — skipping $TAG_TYPE (sub-session recursion guard)"
            clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
            exit 0
        fi
        ;;
esac

case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup)
        hook_debug "Triggering wrap-up sequence"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/generate-summary.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            fi
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            fi
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/wrapup.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "wrap-up started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    review)
        hook_debug "Triggering shallow review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "review started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    deepreview)
        hook_debug "Triggering deep review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "deep"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "deep review started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    docfresh)
        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$ORIG_PROMPT")
        hook_debug "Triggering docfresh for slug=$SLUG"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "$SLUG"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/docfresh.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "docfresh started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    *)
        # No follow-up actions for plain prompts, initprompt, or phaseprompt
        ;;
esac

content.text:
The auto-classifier is blocking edits to `.claude/hooks/stop.sh` — it treats the hooks directory as a self-modification hard block. The first edit (ARG_MAX tempfile fix) slipped through on the ARG_MAX fix, but the larger restructuring was caught.

You need to allow hooks edits. Either run this yourself:

```bash
! cat /home/io/io-dev/io/.claude/hooks/stop.sh
```

and paste in the changes, or add a permission rule for hooks. Here's what the second edit needs to do — I'll give you the exact replacement so you can apply it:

**In `stop.sh`, replace the entire `STEP 2` block** (lines ~85–161 currently) with:

```bash
case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh)
        # Atomic lock acquire: noclobber makes the existence-check and create a
        # single syscall (EEXIST on collision), closing the TOCTOU race window.
        if ! (set -o noclobber; echo "$$:$TAG_TYPE:$(date -Iseconds)" > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK") 2>/dev/null; then
            hook_debug "workflow-action.lock held — skipping $TAG_TYPE (sub-session recursion guard)"
            clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"
            exit 0
        fi
        ;;
esac

case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup)
        hook_debug "Triggering wrap-up sequence"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/generate-summary.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            fi
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            fi
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/wrapup.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "wrap-up started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    review)
        hook_debug "Triggering shallow review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "review started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    deepreview)
        hook_debug "Triggering deep review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "deep"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "deep review started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    docfresh)
        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$ORIG_PROMPT")
        hook_debug "Triggering docfresh for slug=$SLUG"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "$SLUG"
            rm -f "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/docfresh.log" 2>&1 &
        hook_debug "docfresh started in background, pid=$!"
        ;;

    *)
        ;;
esac
```

Key changes:
- One atomic `noclobber` write replaces the check-then-write two-step
- All four `jq -nc '{hookSpecificOutput...}'` calls removed — that's the JSON that was dumping to console
- No duplicate `echo ... > "$WORKFLOW_LOCK"` lines in each branch (lock is already held before we get there)

Also need to update `extract-turn.sh` line 4 (the usage comment) and lines 21-24 to read the prompt from the tempfile path instead of treating `$4` as the prompt string directly:

**In `extract-turn.sh`, replace lines 21-24:**
```bash
TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$1"
START_OFFSET="$2"
LOG_PATH="$3"
ORIG_PROMPT=$(cat "$4")    # $4 is a tempfile path (avoids ARG_MAX on large prompts)
```

And update the usage comment on line 5:
```bash
# Usage: extract-turn.sh TRANSCRIPT_PATH START_OFFSET LOG_PATH PROMPT_TMPFILE
```

Want me to add a permission rule for `.claude/hooks/` so I can apply these directly? Run `/update-config` or tell me and I'll set it up.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# pre-tool-use-edit.sh — PreToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
     3	#
     4	# Fires before file writes. Responsibilities:
     5	#   1. Scan the content being written for common credential/secret patterns
     6	#   2. Block the write if matches are found, with a clear reason
     7	#
     8	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
     9	#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, content, new_string, ...}
    10	#
    11	# Output:
    12	#   - exit 0 with no JSON: write proceeds
    13	#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": write blocked
    14	#
    15	# Note: This is a moderate-aggressiveness scanner. Patterns chosen for low
    16	# false-positive rate. If you find this catching too many false positives,
    17	# adjust SECRET_PATTERNS below. If you find it missing real secrets, ditto.
    18	
    19	set -uo pipefail
    20	
    21	HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-edit"
    22	export HOOK_NAME
    23	
    24	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
    25	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    26	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    27	    source "$LIB_PATH"
    28	fi
    29	
    30	INPUT=$(cat)
    31	
    32	TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
    33	FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
    34	
    35	# Extract content based on which tool fired
    36	CONTENT=""
    37	case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    38	    Write)
    39	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""')
    40	        ;;
    41	    Edit)
    42	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // ""')
    43	        ;;
    44	    MultiEdit)
    45	        # MultiEdit has an edits array, each with new_string
    46	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '[.tool_input.edits[]?.new_string // ""] | join("\n")')
    47	        ;;
    48	    *)
    49	        # Unknown tool variant; allow through
    50	        exit 0
    51	        ;;
    52	esac
    53	
    54	# Skip empty content
    55	if [ -z "$CONTENT" ]; then
    56	    exit 0
    57	fi
    58	
    59	# Skip if the file path looks like a test fixture, documentation, or
    60	# explicitly-marked sample. Reduces false positives for example credentials
    61	# in docs.
    62	case "$FILE_PATH" in
    63	    *test*|*tests*|*spec*|*fixture*|*example*|*sample*|*.md|*.rst|*.txt)
    64	        # These get a lighter scan — only check for very specific real-key prefixes
    65	        SCAN_MODE="light"
    66	        ;;
    67	    *)
    68	        SCAN_MODE="full"
    69	        ;;
    70	esac
    71	
    72	# ============================================================================
    73	# SECRET PATTERNS
    74	# ============================================================================
    75	# Each pattern below is a regex matched case-insensitively against content.
    76	# Patterns are designed to catch real-world credentials with low false positives.
    77	#
    78	# To add a new pattern: append a "pattern|description" line to SECRET_PATTERNS.
    79	# To remove a pattern: delete its line.
    80	# To tune sensitivity: edit the regexes themselves (more specific = fewer FPs).
    81	
    82	SECRET_PATTERNS=(
    83	    # AWS keys
    84	    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    85	    'aws_secret_access_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}|AWS Secret Key'
    86	
    87	    # GitHub tokens
    88	    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    89	    'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}|GitHub fine-grained PAT'
    90	
    91	    # OpenAI
    92	    'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20}T3BlbkFJ[A-Za-z0-9]{20}|OpenAI API key (legacy)'
    93	    'sk-proj-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}|OpenAI project key'
    94	
    95	    # Anthropic
    96	    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
    97	
    98	    # Stripe
    99	    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
   100	    'rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live restricted key'
   101	
   102	    # Google
   103	    'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}|Google API key'
   104	
   105	    # Slack
   106	    'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}|Slack token'
   107	
   108	    # Generic high-entropy patterns near credential identifiers
   109	    # Only used in "full" scan mode to reduce false positives in docs/tests
   110	    'private_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
   111	)
   112	
   113	# Light mode patterns: only obvious real keys, skip identifier-based heuristics
   114	LIGHT_PATTERNS=(
   115	    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
   116	    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
   117	    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
   118	    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
   119	    '-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
   120	)
   121	
   122	if [ "$SCAN_MODE" = "light" ]; then
   123	    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${LIGHT_PATTERNS[@]}")
   124	else
   125	    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}")
   126	fi
   127	
   128	# Scan
   129	MATCHED_PATTERN=""
   130	MATCHED_DESC=""
   131	for entry in "${PATTERNS_TO_USE[@]}"; do
   132	    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
   133	    desc="${entry##*|}"
   134	    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
   135	        MATCHED_PATTERN="$pattern"
   136	        MATCHED_DESC="$desc"
   137	        break
   138	    fi
   139	done
   140	
   141	if [ -n "$MATCHED_PATTERN" ]; then
   142	    # Block the write
   143	    REASON="Secret scanner blocked this write. Detected pattern matching: $MATCHED_DESC. If this is a false positive (example credential, test fixture, etc.), move the file to a path containing 'test', 'example', 'fixture', or 'sample', or modify the secret scanner in .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-edit.sh to exclude this pattern."
   144	
   145	    if [ -n "${HOOK_NAME+x}" ] && command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   146	        hook_debug "BLOCKED: $FILE_PATH matched pattern: $MATCHED_DESC"
   147	    fi
   148	
   149	    jq -nc \
   150	        --arg reason "$REASON" \
   151	        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
   152	    exit 0
   153	fi
   154	
   155	# No secrets detected; allow
   156	exit 0
